_template_instructions.purpose: Define organizational risk acceptability criteria: severity levels, probability levels, and risk matrix
_template_instructions.when_to_fill: Early in risk management process - defines how risks will be evaluated
_template_instructions.how_to_use: 1. Copy to Output/<system>/system/defined/risk-criteria.json, 2. Customize levels for your organization, 3. Remove _template_instructions
_template_instructions.notes: These definitions should align with your organization's risk management policy

severity_levels.name: Negligible
examples: User needs to retry an action

severity_levels.name: Minor
severity_levels.description: Temporary injury or impairment not requiring professional medical intervention
examples: Appointment rescheduled causing minor delay in care
User confusion leading to extra steps

severity_levels.name: Serious
severity_levels.description: Injury or impairment requiring professional medical intervention
examples: Significant delay in care requiring additional treatment
Incorrect practitioner assignment affecting care quality
Missed escalation for moderately urgent case

severity_levels.name: Critical
examples: Failed escalation for urgent case leading to delayed emergency care
Complete loss of patient data during active care
Wrong patient identification in critical workflow

severity_levels.name: Catastrophic
examples: Total system failure during emergency escalation
Systematic error affecting multiple patients in critical care

probability_levels.name: Incredible
probability_levels.description: So unlikely it can be assumed it will not occur
probability_levels.qualitative: Has never occurred in similar systems, multiple barriers exist

probability_levels.name: Remote
probability_levels.qualitative: Rare occurrence in similar systems, strong controls exist

probability_levels.name: Occasional
probability_levels.description: Likely to occur sometime in device lifetime
probability_levels.qualitative: Has occurred in similar systems, controls present but not comprehensive

probability_levels.name: Frequent
probability_levels.qualitative: Expected to occur regularly, insufficient controls

_explanation.acceptable: Risk is acceptable without further action
_explanation.alarp: Risk must be reduced As Low As Reasonably Practicable
_explanation.unacceptable: Risk must be eliminated or reduced before release

visual: S5  |  ALARP   |  ALARP   |  UNACCEPT | UNACCEPT | UNACCEPT |
S4  |  Accept  |  ALARP   |  ALARP    | UNACCEPT | UNACCEPT |
S3  |  Accept  |  Accept  |  ALARP    | ALARP    | UNACCEPT |
S2  |  Accept  |  Accept  |  Accept   | ALARP    | ALARP    |
S1  |  Accept  |  Accept  |  Accept   | Accept   | ALARP    |

benefit_risk_policy.when_applicable: When residual risk is in ALARP region
criteria: Clinical benefit must be documented and substantiated
No practical means to further reduce risk
Benefit must clearly outweigh residual risk
Risk must be disclosed to users
benefit_risk_policy.approval_required: Benefits exceeding risks requires review by: <FILL: role/committee>

overall_residual_risk_policy.evaluation_method: Review all individual residual risks collectively to assess overall device risk
criteria_for_acceptable: ALARP risks have documented benefit-risk analysis
Cumulative risk from multiple ALARP items assessed
